function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent

Deploy a multi-tenant WhatsApp AI support and sales agent. Each client links their WhatsApp Business number → Evolution API instance → webhook to a FastAPI service → AI response back via Evolution.

## Architecture (v3.0.1 — May 2026)

```
Client WhatsApp Business → Evolution API Instance
    → POST /webhook/{instance} (incoming message)
        → FastAPI router → client system prompt + conversation history
            → LightRAG query (entity-aware memory, port 9623)  ← also has PAST CHAT HISTORY context
            → Evolution DB query (past messages for this contact)  ← NEW in v2.1.0
                → AI (DeepSeek with tool-calling → Groq fallback)
                    → Function execution (products, hours, policies)
                    → Second-pass AI with tool results
                        → Evolution API /message/sendText → Customer

                    ← Meanwhile, LightRAG gets batch-imported with past chats
                       via POST /clients/{instance}/import

                    ← Postgres persists clients in "HermesClient" table
                       per-client personality via PATCH /clients/{inst}/personality
```

### Hermes Integration

The WhatsApp agent runs alongside Hermes Agent but NOT as a gateway adapter. In Hermes v0.11.0, `whatsapp_provider: evolution` is a no-op — the gateway only supports the built-in Baileys bridge. Evolution runs as a standalone service:

 | Component | Role | Port |
|-----------|------|------|
| **Evolution API** | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — message send/receive | 8080 |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web interface at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager | (browser) |
| **FastAPI (this service)** | Webhook receiver + client management + history import | 8000 |
| **Evolution Postgres** | Message store (43k+ messages, 409 contacts) — accessed directly | 5432 |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware long-term memory per contact | 9623 |
| **Hermes-lcm plugin** | Lossless DAG context for long conversations | (plugin, no port) |
| **Skill Factory** | Auto-generates improved sales/support scripts | (plugin, reload on /reset) |
| **Traefik** | Public ingress at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud | 443 |

### History Import (New in v2.1.0)

Evolution API v2.3.7 has **no REST endpoint** to retrieve past messages. All `/chat/findMessages`, `/chat/getMessages`, `/message/list` endpoints return 404. The only way to access stored messages is **direct Postgres connection** to Evolution's database.

The agent now does this:
1. Connects to `postgres:5432/evolution` via asyncpg
2. Queries the `Message` table (43,741 messages, back to June 2024) using `"remoteJid"` from the `key` JSONB column
3. Before each AI response, fetches recent past messages for that contact from Evolution's DB
4. Can batch-import entire chat histories into LightRAG via `POST /clients/{instance}/import`

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for full table structure and query patterns.

### LightRAG Integration (Deployed)

LightRAG runs as a Docker container on port 9623 with its own restart policy and volume persistence. Before each AI response, it's queried for entity-aware context:

```python
rag_url = "http://lightrag:9623"  # Docker network; also reachable as localhost outside container
rag_payload = {
    "query": "what does the customer know about? what products have they asked about?",
    "mode": "hybrid"  # local + global graph search
}
rag_response = requests.post(f"{rag_url}/query", json=rag_payload)
```

**What changed this round (May 5):** Migrated from raw systemd process to Docker container with restart-always policy. LightRAG now survives host reboots. Uses DeepSeek (`deepseek-chat`) as LLM backend (via `LLM_BINDING_HOST` env) and `bge-m3` for 1024-dimensional embeddings via Ollama.

**Ollama runs on host** bound to `0.0.0.0:11434` (not `localhost`) so Docker containers can reach it. Models: `nomic-embed-text` (274MB), `bge-m3`, `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. Ollama is also a service with restart policy.

**Cross-session recall:** "María asked about tent X last week" → "María is now asking about camping stoves — connect the interest."

**LightRAG knowledge base** is seeded on startup via `/seed` endpoint with client's products, FAQs, and policies. Tested query for "devolucion" correctly returns 30-day return policy.

**RAM overhead:** LightRAG + Ollama consumes ~2-4GB total. Ensure the VPS has headroom before starting.

## Stack

| Component | What |
|-----------|------|
| **Evolution API** v2.3.7+ | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — one instance per client phone |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web control at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager |
| **FastAPI** webhook receiver | Receives Evolution `onMessage` events, routes per client |
| **pywa** v3.9.0 | Python WhatsApp Cloud API library (installed, for Meta Cloud API) |
| **Traefik** | Exposes webhook receiver at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` |
| **AI** | DeepSeek (primary, with function calling), Groq via LiteLLM (fallback) |
| **PostgreSQL** | Shared — Evolution message store + HermesClient/HermesContact tables |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware memory, per-client workspaces |

## Deployment (Docker Swarm)

```bash
cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent

# Build with version tag
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.1 .

# Deploy with FULL env vars inlined (env_file does NOT work with Swarm deploy)
# The docker-compose.yml must have `environment:` block, NOT `env_file:`
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml whatsapp-ai-stack

# IMPORTANT: env_file is IGNORED by Swarm. All env vars must be inlined
# in the docker-compose.yml `environment:` section.

# To force an update after rebuilding, ALWAYS bump the image tag:
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 .
docker service update --image whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai

# IMPORTANT: `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers
# if the image tag hasn't changed. Always use a new tag.

# If container fails with "password authentication failed for user postgres"
# even after passing PG_DSN in environment, the env vars aren't reaching the container.
# Use `docker service logs whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai --tail 20` to check.
```

### Required Environment Variables (must be inlined in compose)

```yaml
environment:
  EVOLUTION_API_URL: https://evolution.sunstein.cloud
  EVOLUTION_API_KEY: "<full key from container env>"
  DEEPSEEK_API_KEY: "<key>"
  DEEPSEEK_MODEL: deepseek-chat
  PG_DSN: "[REDACTED_DB_URL]

8000"
  MAX_HISTORY: "50"
```

The `.env` file is useful for `docker run` but NOT for `docker stack deploy`.

### pip Install on This Server

```bash
# The system Python on this VPS requires --break-system-packages
pip install --break-system-packages <package>
```

### Traefik Config (after switching to Swarm mode)

The old `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000` must be deleted when moving to Docker Swarm. Swarm-mode Traefik auto-discovers services via `traefik.*` labels on the service. File-path configs override Swarm discovery. Remove:

```bash
rm -f /opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml
```

## Client Onboarding

One API call creates the Evolution instance, sets the webhook, and returns a QR code.

```bash
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Mi Tienda",
    "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
    "mode": "ventas"
  }'
```

Response includes a QR code. Client scans with WhatsApp Business app → instance goes `open` → webhook active.

### Modes (built-in prompts)

| Mode | Use |
|------|-----|
| `default` | General customer service |
| `ventas` | Sales assistant — products, pricing, buying process |
| `soporte` | Technical support — returns, warranties, tracking |
| Custom `system_prompt` | Override with any text |

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Service status |
| `/clients` | GET | List all clients from Postgres |
| `/clients` | POST | Onboard new client (creates Evolution instance + webhook + Postgres row + optional LightRAG workspace) |
| `/clients/cloud` | POST | Create Cloud API client (requires CLOUD_API_TOKEN + CLOUD_PHONE_ID env vars). Returns QR or pairing code instructions. |
| `/clients/{instance}` | GET | Client details from Postgres |
| `/clients/{instance}` | DELETE | Remove client from DB + Evolution |
| `/clients/{instance}/personality` | PATCH | Update mode/system_prompt/repo_path |
| `/clients/{instance}/qr` | GET | Regenerate QR code for re-pairing |
| `/webhook/{instance}` | POST | Evolution fires this on each incoming message |
| `/clients/{instance}/chats` | GET | List all contacts with message counts & date ranges |
| `/clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}` | GET | Get past messages for a specific contact (bare number or full JID) |
| `/clients/{instance}/import` | POST | Batch-import past chat history into LightRAG memory |

### POST /clients payload

```json
{
  "name": "string (required) — client display name",
  "phone": "string (required) — raw digits, no + or spaces",
  "mode": "string (optional) — default|ventas|soporte",
  "system_prompt": "string (optional) — overrides mode",
  "repo_path": "string (optional) — path to client's repo for knowledge base",
  "lightrag_workspace": "string (optional) — workspace name for tenant isolation"
}
```

Response includes `lightrag_workspace` — used for tenant-isolated memory in LightRAG. If not specified, auto-generated from phone number.

### PATCH /clients/{instance}/personality

Update a client's AI personality without recreating the instance:

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/client-store-1234/personality \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"mode": "ventas", "repo_path": "elviajero-comercio"}'
```

Valid fields: `mode`, `system_prompt`, `repo_path`.

### POST /clients/cloud

For WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) instead of Evolution Baileys. Requires Meta Business setup:

```json
{
  "name": "Mi Tienda",
  "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
  "mode": "ventas"
}
```

If CLOUD_API_TOKEN is configured, attempts Evolution instance creation first. Falls back to pairing code instructions. Cloud API clients get their own LightRAG workspace.

## AI Backend

The service tries providers in order:

1. **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-chat` via `api.deepseek.com/v1`) — $0.14/M input tokens (primary, low latency). **Supports function calling** with `tools` parameter.
2. **Groq** (`groq-llama` via local LiteLLM proxy on :4000) — free tier, 6 models available, no function calling.
3. Returns error message if all fail.

### Tool Calling (v3.0+)

The agent uses DeepSeek's native function calling with a two-pass execution:

```python
# First call: LLM decides which tool(s) to call
tools = [
    {
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "lookup_product",
            "description": "Search products by name or category",
            "parameters": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}},
                "required": ["query"]
            }
        }
    },
    # ... get_business_hours, get_shipping_policy, get_return_policy
]

# Second call: LLM synthesizes response with tool results
messages.append(tool_result_message)
resp2 = await client.post(..., json={"messages": messages})
```

Supported tools: `lookup_product`, `get_business_hours`, `get_shipping_policy`, `get_return_policy`. Tools are defined in code, not fetched from a registry.

### Direct Message Sending via Instance Token

Evolution API v2.3.7 has two auth mechanisms. The global API key may be restricted — many endpoints return 401. For sending messages, use the **instance-specific token** stored in Postgres:

```bash
docker exec postgres_postgres.* psql -U postgres -d evolution \
  -c "SELECT token FROM \"Instance\" WHERE name='hermes-whatsapp';"

curl -X POST "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/message/sendText/hermes-whatsapp" \
  -H "apikey: $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"number": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "text": "Hello", "delay": 1000}'
```

Response includes `"status": "PENDING"` when instance is reconnecting. Message delivers once reconnected.

**Fallback chain verification:** All 3 providers tested and confirmed working in this session. LiteLLM/Groq was reconfigured from OpenRouter to reduce costs. Cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic to DeepSeek.

Before each AI call, the service queries LightRAG for entity-aware context about the customer and conversation topic. This context is prepended to the system prompt. The LightRAG knowledge base is pre-seeded with client products, FAQs, and policies.

Conversation history is stored per client (in-memory dict, last 50 messages). Postgres persistence is planned.

### Session Context Chain (Order of Operations)

1. Receive webhook message from Evolution
2. Filter `fromMe: true` messages (echo prevention) and group messages unless configured otherwise
3. Check in-memory conversation history for this remoteJid (last 50 messages)
4. **NEW: Query Evolution Postgres DB for this contact's past messages (last 15 messages, not in memory)**
5. Query LightRAG for relevant entity context about customer + topic
6. Build prompt: system_prompt + RAG context + Evolution history + conversation memory + new message
7. Call AI (try DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain)
8. Send response via Evolution API sendText
9. Store message + response in conversation history
10. Optionally update LightRAG with new entities from the exchange

### Batch Import (Post-Deployment)

After deploying a new instance, import its past messages into LightRAG:

```bash
# Import top 50 individual chats (500 messages each)
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/hermes-whatsapp/import \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"max_per_chat": 500, "max_chats": 50}'
```

This feeds ~25,000 messages as entity-aware LightRAG chunks. Import is idempotent — re-running creates additional chunks but doesn't corrupt existing memory.

## Evolution Manager UI

Accessible at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager`. Login with the **full** API key (retrieved from the container env, not the truncated .env version). From the UI you can:
- View/QR-reconnect instances
- Browse chats and contacts
- Configure webhooks and integrations

See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.

## Postgres Column Quoting (CRITICAL)

Evolution's Prisma schema uses camelCase column names. Postgres lowercases unquoted identifiers. **Every** camelCase reference in SQL must be double-quoted:

```sql
-- WRONG: pushName becomes pushname → error
SELECT pushName FROM "Message" WHERE pushName IS NOT NULL;

-- RIGHT: 
SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message" WHERE "pushName" IS NOT NULL;
```

**Bug we found:** Both `_enrich_chat_name()` and `_fetch_chat_messages()` had unquoted `pushName` in their SQL. Symptoms: `column "pushname" does not exist`. Fix all locations with a grep:

```bash
grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py
# Should only show "pushName" references
```

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for complete quoting reference.

## Pitfalls

1. **Evolution instance creation requires `integration: WHATSAPP-BAILEYS`** — without it the API returns `400 Invalid integration`
2. **Webhook URL must be publicly reachable** — Evolution runs in Docker overlay, can't reach `localhost`. Must use Traefik public URL (e.g. `[REDACTED_WEBHOOK] **QR codes expire in ~45 seconds** — be ready to scan immediately. Regenerate with `GET /clients/{instance}/qr`
4. **WhatsApp max 4 linked devices** per phone number. Client may need to remove old links
5. **Manager UI needs full-length API key**: The `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` shown in `docker service inspect` is truncated with `***`. The actual key is longer. Get it via `docker exec <container> sh -c 'echo $AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY'`. The Manager login at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager` uses this full key.
6. **Swarm ignores `env_file`**: Docker Swarm deploy does NOT read `.env` files from docker-compose.yml. All environment variables must be passed inline under `environment:` in the compose file. If PG_DSN or API keys are only in `.env`, the container starts but fails (exit code 3, password auth error).
7. **Postgres camelCase columns must be double-quoted**: Evolution's tables use mixed-case columns like `"pushName"`, `"messageTimestamp"`, `"remoteJid"`. In raw SQL passed to asyncpg, these MUST be wrapped in double quotes: `SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message"`. Without quotes, Postgres lowercases them to `pushname` and the query fails with `column "pushname" does not exist`.
8. **Instance shows `open` but messages stay PENDING**: If an instance shows `connectionStatus=open` but messages remain `PENDING` status, the session is stale. Fix: logout → get fresh QR → re-scan. `DELETE /instance/logout/{name}` then `GET /instance/connect/{name}` to generate new QR.
9. **Instance-specific tokens vs global key**: The global `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` works for instance management (create, delete, fetch) and Manager UI. For sending messages, use the per-instance token from the `"Instance"` table or the `token` field in Evolution API responses. The per-instance token is a UUID format (e.g. `5C[REDACTED_PHONE]DE-...`).
10. **Image tag must change on each deploy**: Docker Swarm doesn't detect local image changes for the same tag. Always bump the tag (e.g. v2.1.0 → v2.1.1) when rebuilding. Use `docker service update --force --image <name>:<new-tag> <service>` for immediate rollout.
5. **Swarm image tags must be bumped for updates** — `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers when the image tag is identical. Always use a new tag (`v2.1.0` → `v2.1.1`). See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
6. **Traefik file config blocks Swarm discovery** — when moving from standalone to Swarm, delete `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` or Swarm labels are ignored. File providers take precedence over Swarm auto-discovery. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
7. **Evolution API v2.3.7 has NO pairing code endpoint** — `POST /instance/pairingCode/*`, `POST /instance/requestPairingCode/*`, `PUT /instance/connect/*` all return 404. The ONLY connection method is QR scanning via `GET /instance/connect/{name}`. WhatsApp Business pairing codes require Evolution API v2.4+.
8. **Postgres column quoting** — Prisma-generated Evolution tables use camelCase columns. Postgres lowercases unquoted column names. Double-quote ALL references: `"pushName"`. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes in BOTH SELECT and WHERE clauses. See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md`.
9. **Import returns "column pushname does not exist"** — Same quoting issue as above. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes in both SELECT and WHERE. Check ALL locations: `_enrich_chat_name()`, `_fetch_chat_messages()`, and any other query reading `pushName`, `messageTimestamp`, `remoteJid`, `instanceId`. Run grep before deploying.
10. **Sending messages requires instance token** — The global API key often returns 401 for sendText. Get the instance token from Postgres (`SELECT token FROM "Instance" WHERE name='<name>'`) and use that as `apikey` header. Response status `PENDING` is normal — message delivers once the instance reconnects.
11. **Swarm deployment: "Bad Gateway" after redeploy** — Traefik takes ~30s to refresh its service discovery after a Swarm container restarts. The old Traefik static file config at `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` overrides Swarm discovery — must be deleted when moving to Swarm mode. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
12. **Evolution Manager UI requires full API key** — The Manager login at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager` stores the entered key in localStorage. If the .env key is truncated, the Manager shows 401 errors. Get the full key from the container's environment variable (`AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY`), NOT from the .env file. See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

When an Evolution API instance shows `connectionStatus: connecting` with `disconnectionReasonCode: 401`, it means the WhatsApp session token expired or the device was logged out.

## Symptoms

- Postgres: `connectionStatus: connecting`, `disconnectionReasonCode: 401`
- Evolution API: `fetchInstances` shows `ownerJid` populated but `disconnectionObject` has `"statusCode": 401, "message": "Log out instance: <name>"`
- Messages sent via this instance's token return `PENDING` and never deliver
- Container logs show QR code being printed on each restart (stale QR — won't work)

## Repair Steps

1. **Log out the stale instance:**
```bash
EVO_KEY="<full-manager-key>"
INSTANCE_NAME="hermes-whatsapp"
curl -s -X DELETE "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/logout/$INSTANCE_NAME" \
  -H "apikey: $EVO_KEY" | python3 -m json.tool
```

2. **Get a fresh QR code:**
```bash
curl -s -X GET "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/$INSTANCE_NAME" \
  -H "apikey: $EVO_KEY" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
code = d.get('qrcode', {}).get('code', '') or d.get('code', '')
base64 = d.get('qrcode', {}).get('base64', '') or d.get('base64', '')
if code:
    print(f'QR Pairing Code: {code}')
if base64:
    print(f'Base64: {base64[:80]}...')
print(json.dumps(d, indent=2))
"
```

3. **Scan QR** with WhatsApp (Linked Devices → Link a Device). The instance transitions to `open` within seconds.

4. **Verify:**
```bash
curl -s "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/fetchInstances" \
  -H "apikey: $EVO_KEY" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
for inst in json.load(sys.stdin):
    print(f'{inst[\"name\"]:25} | {inst[\"connectionStatus\"]:12} | {inst.get(\"ownerJid\",\"\")}')
"
```

## Notes

- 401 disconnections happen when WhatsApp Web session expires (~2-4 weeks) or when the phone's linked devices list is full (max 4).
- After relinking, the instance token stays the same — no need to update webhook URLs or client configs.
- The `hermes-whatsapp` instance has 44k+ messages — relinking preserves the message history in Postgres.
"}

# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent

Deploy a multi-tenant WhatsApp AI support and sales agent. Each client links their WhatsApp Business number → Evolution API instance → webhook to a FastAPI service → AI response back via Evolution.

## Architecture (v3.0.1 — May 2026)

```
Client WhatsApp Business → Evolution API Instance
    → POST /webhook/{instance} (incoming message)
        → FastAPI router → client system prompt + conversation history
            → LightRAG query (entity-aware memory, port 9623)  ← also has PAST CHAT HISTORY context
            → Evolution DB query (past messages for this contact)  ← NEW in v2.1.0
                → AI (DeepSeek with tool-calling → Groq fallback)
                    → Function execution (products, hours, policies)
                    → Second-pass AI with tool results
                        → Evolution API /message/sendText → Customer

                    ← Meanwhile, LightRAG gets batch-imported with past chats
                       via POST /clients/{instance}/import

                    ← Postgres persists clients in "HermesClient" table
                       per-client personality via PATCH /clients/{inst}/personality
```

### Hermes Integration

The WhatsApp agent runs alongside Hermes Agent but NOT as a gateway adapter. In Hermes v0.11.0, `whatsapp_provider: evolution` is a no-op — the gateway only supports the built-in Baileys bridge. Evolution runs as a standalone service:

 | Component | Role | Port |
|-----------|------|------|
| **Evolution API** | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — message send/receive | 8080 |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web interface at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager | (browser) |
| **FastAPI (this service)** | Webhook receiver + client management + history import | 8000 |
| **Evolution Postgres** | Message store (43k+ messages, 409 contacts) — accessed directly | 5432 |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware long-term memory per contact | 9623 |
| **Hermes-lcm plugin** | Lossless DAG context for long conversations | (plugin, no port) |
| **Skill Factory** | Auto-generates improved sales/support scripts | (plugin, reload on /reset) |
| **Traefik** | Public ingress at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud | 443 |

### History Import (New in v2.1.0)

Evolution API v2.3.7 has **no REST endpoint** to retrieve past messages. All `/chat/findMessages`, `/chat/getMessages`, `/message/list` endpoints return 404. The only way to access stored messages is **direct Postgres connection** to Evolution's database.

The agent now does this:
1. Connects to `postgres:5432/evolution` via asyncpg
2. Queries the `Message` table (43,741 messages, back to June 2024) using `"remoteJid"` from the `key` JSONB column
3. Before each AI response, fetches recent past messages for that contact from Evolution's DB
4. Can batch-import entire chat histories into LightRAG via `POST /clients/{instance}/import`

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for full table structure and query patterns.

### LightRAG Integration (Deployed)

LightRAG runs as a Docker container on port 9623 with its own restart policy and volume persistence. Before each AI response, it's queried for entity-aware context:

```python
rag_url = "http://lightrag:9623"  # Docker network; also reachable as localhost outside container
rag_payload = {
    "query": "what does the customer know about? what products have they asked about?",
    "mode": "hybrid"  # local + global graph search
}
rag_response = requests.post(f"{rag_url}/query", json=rag_payload)
```

**What changed this round (May 5):** Migrated from raw systemd process to Docker container with restart-always policy. LightRAG now survives host reboots. Uses DeepSeek (`deepseek-chat`) as LLM backend (via `LLM_BINDING_HOST` env) and `bge-m3` for 1024-dimensional embeddings via Ollama.

**Ollama runs on host** bound to `0.0.0.0:11434` (not `localhost`) so Docker containers can reach it. Models: `nomic-embed-text` (274MB), `bge-m3`, `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. Ollama is also a service with restart policy.

**Cross-session recall:** "María asked about tent X last week" → "María is now asking about camping stoves — connect the interest."

**LightRAG knowledge base** is seeded on startup via `/seed` endpoint with client's products, FAQs, and policies. Tested query for "devolucion" correctly returns 30-day return policy.

**RAM overhead:** LightRAG + Ollama consumes ~2-4GB total. Ensure the VPS has headroom before starting.

## Stack

| Component | What |
|-----------|------|
|-----------|------|
| **Evolution API** v2.3.7+ | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — one instance per client phone |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | Web control at https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager |
| **FastAPI** webhook receiver | Receives Evolution `onMessage` events, routes per client |
| **pywa** v3.9.0 | Python WhatsApp Cloud API library (installed, for Meta Cloud API) |
| **Traefik** | Exposes webhook receiver at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` |
| **AI** | DeepSeek (primary, with function calling), Groq via LiteLLM (fallback) |
| **PostgreSQL** | Shared — Evolution message store + HermesClient/HermesContact tables |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware memory, per-client workspaces |

## Deployment (Docker Swarm)

```bash
cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent

# Build with version tag
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.1 .

# Deploy with FULL env vars inlined (env_file does NOT work with Swarm deploy)
# The docker-compose.yml must have `environment:` block, NOT `env_file:`
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml whatsapp-ai-stack

# IMPORTANT: env_file is IGNORED by Swarm. All env vars must be inlined
# in the docker-compose.yml `environment:` section.

# To force an update after rebuilding, ALWAYS bump the image tag:
docker build -t whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 .
docker service update --image whatsapp-ai-agent:v3.0.2 whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai

# IMPORTANT: `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers
# if the image tag hasn't changed. Always use a new tag.

# If container fails with "password authentication failed for user postgres"
# even after passing PG_DSN in environment, the env vars aren't reaching the container.
# Use `docker service logs whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai --tail 20` to check.
```

### Required Environment Variables (must be inlined in compose)

```yaml
environment:
  EVOLUTION_API_URL: https://evolution.sunstein.cloud
  EVOLUTION_API_KEY: "<full key from container env>"
  DEEPSEEK_API_KEY: "<key>"
  DEEPSEEK_MODEL: deepseek-chat
  PG_DSN: "[REDACTED_DB_URL]

8000"
  MAX_HISTORY: "50"
```

The `.env` file is useful for `docker run` but NOT for `docker stack deploy`.

### pip Install on This Server

```bash
# The system Python on this VPS requires --break-system-packages
pip install --break-system-packages <package>
```

### Traefik Config (after switching to Swarm mode)

The old `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000` must be deleted when moving to Docker Swarm. Swarm-mode Traefik auto-discovers services via `traefik.*` labels on the service. File-path configs override Swarm discovery. Remove:

```bash
rm -f /opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml
```

## Client Onboarding

One API call creates the Evolution instance, sets the webhook, and returns a QR code.

```bash
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Mi Tienda",
    "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
    "mode": "ventas"
  }'
```

Response includes a QR code. Client scans with WhatsApp Business app → instance goes `open` → webhook active.

### Modes (built-in prompts)

| Mode | Use |
|------|-----|
| `default` | General customer service |
| `ventas` | Sales assistant — products, pricing, buying process |
| `soporte` | Technical support — returns, warranties, tracking |
| Custom `system_prompt` | Override with any text |

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Service status |
| `/clients` | GET | List all clients from Postgres |
| `/clients` | POST | Onboard new client (creates Evolution instance + webhook + Postgres row + optional LightRAG workspace) |
| `/clients/cloud` | POST | Create Cloud API client (requires CLOUD_API_TOKEN + CLOUD_PHONE_ID env vars). Returns QR or pairing code instructions. |
| `/clients/{instance}` | GET | Client details from Postgres |
| `/clients/{instance}` | DELETE | Remove client from DB + Evolution |
| `/clients/{instance}/personality` | PATCH | Update mode/system_prompt/repo_path |
| `/clients/{instance}/qr` | GET | Regenerate QR code for re-pairing |
| `/webhook/{instance}` | POST | Evolution fires this on each incoming message |
| `/clients/{instance}/chats` | GET | List all contacts with message counts & date ranges |
| `/clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}` | GET | Get past messages for a specific contact (bare number or full JID) |
| `/clients/{instance}/import` | POST | Batch-import past chat history into LightRAG memory |

### POST /clients payload

```json
{
  "name": "string (required) — client display name",
  "phone": "string (required) — raw digits, no + or spaces",
  "mode": "string (optional) — default|ventas|soporte",
  "system_prompt": "string (optional) — overrides mode",
  "repo_path": "string (optional) — path to client's repo for knowledge base",
  "lightrag_workspace": "string (optional) — workspace name for tenant isolation"
}
```

Response includes `lightrag_workspace` — used for tenant-isolated memory in LightRAG. If not specified, auto-generated from phone number.

### PATCH /clients/{instance}/personality

Update a client's AI personality without recreating the instance:

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/client-store-1234/personality \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"mode": "ventas", "repo_path": "elviajero-comercio"}'
```

Valid fields: `mode`, `system_prompt`, `repo_path`.

### POST /clients/cloud

For WhatsApp Cloud API (Meta) instead of Evolution Baileys. Requires Meta Business setup:

```json
{
  "name": "Mi Tienda",
  "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
  "mode": "ventas"
}
```

If CLOUD_API_TOKEN is configured, attempts Evolution instance creation first. Falls back to pairing code instructions. Cloud API clients get their own LightRAG workspace.

## AI Backend

The service tries providers in order:

1. **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-chat` via `api.deepseek.com/v1`) — $0.14/M input tokens (primary, low latency). **Supports function calling** with `tools` parameter.
2. **Groq** (`groq-llama` via local LiteLLM proxy on :4000) — free tier, 6 models available, no function calling.
3. Returns error message if all fail.

### Tool Calling (v3.0+)

The agent uses DeepSeek's native function calling with a two-pass execution:

```python
# First call: LLM decides which tool(s) to call
tools = [
    {
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "lookup_product",
            "description": "Search products by name or category",
            "parameters": {
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}},
                "required": ["query"]
            }
        }
    },
    # ... get_business_hours, get_shipping_policy, get_return_policy
]

# Second call: LLM synthesizes response with tool results
messages.append(tool_result_message)
resp2 = await client.post(..., json={"messages": messages})
```

Supported tools: `lookup_product`, `get_business_hours`, `get_shipping_policy`, `get_return_policy`. Tools are defined in code, not fetched from a registry.

### Direct Message Sending via Instance Token

Evolution API v2.3.7 has two auth mechanisms. The global API key may be restricted — many endpoints return 401. For sending messages, use the **instance-specific token** stored in Postgres:

```bash
docker exec postgres_postgres.* psql -U postgres -d evolution \
  -c "SELECT token FROM \"Instance\" WHERE name='hermes-whatsapp';"

curl -X POST "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/message/sendText/hermes-whatsapp" \
  -H "apikey: $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"number": "[REDACTED_PHONE]", "text": "Hello", "delay": 1000}'
```

Response includes `"status": "PENDING"` when instance is reconnecting. Message delivers once reconnected.

**Fallback chain verification:** All 3 providers tested and confirmed working in this session. LiteLLM/Groq was reconfigured from OpenRouter to reduce costs. Cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic to DeepSeek.

Before each AI call, the service queries LightRAG for entity-aware context about the customer and conversation topic. This context is prepended to the system prompt. The LightRAG knowledge base is pre-seeded with client products, FAQs, and policies.

Conversation history is stored per client (in-memory dict, last 50 messages). Postgres persistence is planned.

### Session Context Chain (Order of Operations)

1. Receive webhook message from Evolution
2. Filter `fromMe: true` messages (echo prevention) and group messages unless configured otherwise
3. Check in-memory conversation history for this remoteJid (last 50 messages)
4. **NEW: Query Evolution Postgres DB for this contact's past messages (last 15 messages, not in memory)**
5. Query LightRAG for relevant entity context about customer + topic
6. Build prompt: system_prompt + RAG context + Evolution history + conversation memory + new message
7. Call AI (try DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain)
8. Send response via Evolution API sendText
9. Store message + response in conversation history
10. Optionally update LightRAG with new entities from the exchange

### Batch Import (Post-Deployment)

After deploying a new instance, import its past messages into LightRAG:

```bash
# Import top 50 individual chats (500 messages each)
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/hermes-whatsapp/import \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"max_per_chat": 500, "max_chats": 50}'
```

This feeds ~25,000 messages as entity-aware LightRAG chunks. Import is idempotent — re-running creates additional chunks but doesn't corrupt existing memory.

## Evolution Manager UI

Accessible at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager`. Login with the **full** API key (retrieved from the container env, not the truncated .env version). From the UI you can:
- View/QR-reconnect instances
- Browse chats and contacts
- Configure webhooks and integrations

See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.

## Postgres Column Quoting (CRITICAL)

Evolution's Prisma schema uses camelCase column names. Postgres lowercases unquoted identifiers. **Every** camelCase reference in SQL must be double-quoted:

```sql
-- WRONG: pushName becomes pushname → error
SELECT pushName FROM "Message" WHERE pushName IS NOT NULL;

-- RIGHT: 
SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message" WHERE "pushName" IS NOT NULL;
```

**Bug we found:** Both `_enrich_chat_name()` and `_fetch_chat_messages()` had unquoted `pushName` in their SQL. Symptoms: `column "pushname" does not exist`. Fix all locations with a grep:

```bash
grep -n 'SELECT.*pushName\|WHERE.*pushName' app/main.py
# Should only show "pushName" references
```

See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md` for complete quoting reference.

## Pitfalls

1. **`connectionStatus: connecting` is NORMAL for unpaired instances** — instances with `connecting` status are simply waiting for QR scan. They have NOT failed. Hermes-whatsapp shows `connecting` even when disconnected (401). The `connecting` state is reported by both Postgres `"connectionStatus"` and the Evolution API `fetchInstances` response. Do NOT assume the instance is broken unless you see `disconnectionReasonCode` or `close` status. Running instances show the QR code in logs but respond to `fetchInstances` with `connectionStatus: connecting`.

2. **Evolution instance creation requires `integration: WHATSAPP-BAILEYS`** — without it the API returns `400 Invalid integration`
2. **Webhook URL must be publicly reachable** — Evolution runs in Docker overlay, can't reach `localhost`. Must use Traefik public URL (e.g. `[REDACTED_WEBHOOK] **QR codes expire in ~45 seconds** — be ready to scan immediately. Regenerate with `GET /clients/{instance}/qr`
4. **WhatsApp max 4 linked devices** per phone number. Client may need to remove old links
5. **Manager UI needs full-length API key**: The `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` shown in `docker service inspect` is truncated with `***`. The actual key is longer. Get it via `docker exec <container> sh -c 'echo $AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY'`. The Manager login at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager` uses this full key.
6. **Swarm ignores `env_file`**: Docker Swarm deploy does NOT read `.env` files from docker-compose.yml. All environment variables must be passed inline under `environment:` in the compose file. If PG_DSN or API keys are only in `.env`, the container starts but fails (exit code 3, password auth error).
7. **Postgres camelCase columns must be double-quoted**: Evolution's tables use mixed-case columns like `"pushName"`, `"messageTimestamp"`, `"remoteJid"`. In raw SQL passed to asyncpg, these MUST be wrapped in double quotes: `SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message"`. Without quotes, Postgres lowercases them to `pushname` and the query fails with `column "pushname" does not exist`.
8. **Instance shows `open` but messages stay PENDING**: If an instance shows `connectionStatus=open` but messages remain `PENDING` status, the session is stale. Fix: logout → get fresh QR → re-scan. `DELETE /instance/logout/{name}` then `GET /instance/connect/{name}` to generate new QR.
9. **Instance-specific tokens vs global key**: The global `AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY` works for instance management (create, delete, fetch) and Manager UI. For sending messages, use the per-instance token from the `"Instance"` table or the `token` field in Evolution API responses. The per-instance token is a UUID format (e.g. `5C[REDACTED_PHONE]DE-...`).
10. **Image tag must change on each deploy**: Docker Swarm doesn't detect local image changes for the same tag. Always bump the tag (e.g. v2.1.0 → v2.1.1) when rebuilding. Use `docker service update --force --image <name>:<new-tag> <service>` for immediate rollout.
5. **Swarm image tags must be bumped for updates** — `docker service update --force` does NOT restart containers when the image tag is identical. Always use a new tag (`v2.1.0` → `v2.1.1`). See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
6. **Traefik file config blocks Swarm discovery** — when moving from standalone to Swarm, delete `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` or Swarm labels are ignored. File providers take precedence over Swarm auto-discovery. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
7. **Evolution API v2.3.7 has NO pairing code endpoint** — `POST /instance/pairingCode/*`, `POST /instance/requestPairingCode/*`, `PUT /instance/connect/*` all return 404. The ONLY connection method is QR scanning via `GET /instance/connect/{name}`. WhatsApp Business pairing codes require Evolution API v2.4+.
8. **Postgres column quoting** — Prisma-generated Evolution tables use camelCase columns. Postgres lowercases unquoted column names. Double-quote ALL references: `"pushName"`. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes in BOTH SELECT and WHERE clauses. See `references/evolution-postgres-schema.md`.
9. **Import returns "column pushname does not exist"** — Same quoting issue as above. Every camelCase reference needs double quotes in both SELECT and WHERE. Check ALL locations: `_enrich_chat_name()`, `_fetch_chat_messages()`, and any other query reading `pushName`, `messageTimestamp`, `remoteJid`, `instanceId`. Run grep before deploying.
10. **Sending messages requires instance token** — The global API key often returns 401 for sendText. Get the instance token from Postgres (`SELECT token FROM "Instance" WHERE name='<name>'`) and use that as `apikey` header. Response status `PENDING` is normal — message delivers once the instance reconnects.
11. **Swarm deployment: "Bad Gateway" after redeploy** — Traefik takes ~30s to refresh its service discovery after a Swarm container restarts. The old Traefik static file config at `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` overrides Swarm discovery — must be deleted when moving to Swarm mode. See `references/swarm-deployment-pitfalls.md`.
12. **Evolution Manager UI requires full API key** — The Manager login at `https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/manager` stores the entered key in localStorage. If the .env key is truncated, the Manager shows 401 errors. Get the full key from the container's environment variable (`AUTHENTICATION_API_KEY`), NOT from the .env file. See `references/evolution-manager-ui.md`.
